consistency.notes: The spell description, components, and effects are mechanically consistent with established D&D 5e spell conventions. The dual-direction functionality (inward vs. outward protection) is clearly defined.

quality.notes: This spell offers strong, defined tactical utility (area denial and protection against specific creature types). The cost (100gp material component consumed) is appropriate for a 3rd-level defensive/control spell with a long duration and significant area of effect.

mechanics.casting_time: 1 minute (long setup time implies it's for preparation, not combat reaction)
mechanics.range: 10 feet (short range means caster must be close to the intended area)
mechanics.components: V, S, M (costly and consumed, reinforcing its importance)
mechanics.primary_effect: Area denial/restriction targeting specific creature types (celestials, elementals, fey, fiends, undead).
key_mechanics: Forced saving throw (Charisma) to bypass boundary via teleportation/planar travel.
Disadvantage on attack rolls made by the creature against targets inside.
Immunity to mental/control effects (charmed, frightened, possessed) from the creature while inside.
mechanics.scaling: Linear increase in duration (+1 hour per spell level above 3rd), which is standard and effective scaling for duration-based spells.

analysis.suggestions: None. The spell is well-balanced and clearly written.
